paper_id: Weigand et al. - 2021 - An artifact ontology for design science research

models.model_label: General artifact ontology (UFO-grounded, OntoUML) for Design Science Research

evidence_quotes.quote: The goal of this conceptual paper is to introduce a general artifact ontology for use in DSR, rooted in the foundational ontology UFO [2,3] and from there develop an ontology of IS artifacts.
This conceptual paper provides an ontological analysis of the notion of artifact grounded in the foundational ontology UFO.
The theoretical contribution of this paper is to work out an ontological account of the artifact and the related design process.

In this paper, we have proposed some important distinctions, in particular the distinction between a technical object and artifact and the distinction between technical artifacts and non-technical artifacts.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly frames the ontology as an instrument to clarify, define, and conceptualize the artifact notion (signal patterns: 'clarify', 'define', 'conceptualize', 'ontological analysis'). Validation is based on competency questions about artifact concepts, which is meaning/knowledge clarification of the domain.

evidence_quotes.quote: We also derive practical implications for Design Science Research.
Based on our ontological analysis, we can give some practical implications for the designer and design researcher in the form of guidelines.

uncertain_goals.reason: The paper derives guidelines from the ontology, but the ontology itself is not used as a decision/option-analysis tool; the guidelines are downstream advice rather than evidence that the model supported decision-making, evaluation, or option comparison.

evidence_quotes.quote: This account can be useful for the further development of DSR methods.
As far as DSR is concerned, it is interesting to look at the impact of this artifact ontology on design methodologies.

uncertain_goals.reason: Potential future use for developing DSR methods is mentioned, but framed as future/possible utility rather than an actual implementation or explicit intended purpose of the model to build a system or artifact, so it falls under the negative signal 'future work / proposed only'.
